_note: Test 05 — built on public-domain VOA Learning English episodes. Audio streamed from VOA's CDN; questions are original IP. Run: php artisan ingest:voa-listening database/seeders/data/voa_test_05.json --force
title: VOA — Eagle Eyes, Wildlife, Assistive AI, Toyota Woven City
content: Section 1: 'eagle eyes' idioms · Section 2: aggressive monkeys at Angkor Wat · Section 3: assistive AI for students · Section 4: Toyota's robotics city
attribution: Audio courtesy of VOA Learning English (learningenglish.voanews.com) — public domain. Questions © IELTS Band AI.
license: public domain (US federal government work, commercial reuse permitted with attribution)

questions.question: The host says a 'bird's-eye view' means an overall, or big-picture, ________.
To describe a person who notices fine details, the host uses the idiom 'eagle ________'.
Another idiom for someone very observant is '________-eyed'.
What general term does the host use for birds with sharp vision such as eagles, hawks and owls?
Raptors have ________ vision, meaning both eyes can focus on a single object.
What does the host say is special about the bones around an eagle's eyes?
options: A. They make the eyes appear larger
B. They protect the eyes from bright sunlight
C. They allow the eyes to move backward
D. They keep water out during dives
questions.answer: B. They protect the eyes from bright sunlight

questions.question: According to the Oxford English Dictionary, the first written use of 'eagle eye' dates from the year ________.
The first written use of the phrase 'eagle eye' is credited to a Roman Catholic ________ named Thomas Stapleton.
Which source is given for the biology facts about raptors?
options: A. The Raptor Center at the University of Minnesota
questions.answer: A. The Raptor Center at the University of Minnesota

questions.question: How does the host suggest using the phrase 'bird's-eye view' at work?
options: A. To describe a final report
B. To describe the big-picture questions to ask at the start of a project
C. To describe a small team
D. To describe a single task
questions.answer: B. To describe the big-picture questions to ask at the start of a project

questions.question: The feature is about wild ________ monkeys around Angkor Wat in Cambodia.
Angkor Wat became a UNESCO World Heritage Site in the year ________.
About how long ago did building work on the Angkor Wat complex begin?
Historically, Angkor Wat was the capital of the ________ Empire.
In which part of Cambodia is Angkor Wat located?
About how many foreign tourists visited Angkor Wat last year?
options: A. More than one hundred thousand
B. More than five hundred thousand

questions.question: The organisation that runs the temple complex is called the ________ National Authority.
According to officials, what is the main reason the monkeys have become aggressive?
options: B. Being fed by tourists, including YouTube influencers
C. A drop in their natural food supply
questions.answer: B. Being fed by tourists, including YouTube influencers

questions.question: Which of the following damaging behaviours is NOT mentioned in the feature?
options: D. Attacking other animals in the park
questions.answer: D. Attacking other animals in the park

questions.question: As one response to the problem, officials have announced a ________ of monkeys in public areas.
AI-powered assistive technology is helping students who have ________, vision problems, or speech disorders.
Which U.S. federal law requires schools to provide assistive technology?
options: A. The Americans with Disabilities Act
B. The Individuals with Disabilities Education Act
C. The No Child Left Behind Act
D. The Every Student Succeeds Act
questions.answer: B. The Individuals with Disabilities Education Act

questions.question: The AI research institute mentioned is based at the University at ________ in New York.
Which U.S. agency funds the National AI Institute for Exceptional Education?
options: D. The National Institutes of Health

questions.question: What does 14-year-old Ben Snyder use the 'Question AI' app for?
Using the AI tool, Ben Snyder can finish an outline in about ________ minutes instead of 90.
What achievement did Makenzie Gilkison earn after starting to use AI tools?
options: A. She won a national writing prize
B. She was named to the National Junior Honor Society
C. She was offered a university scholarship
D. She completed a public-speaking course
questions.answer: B. She was named to the National Junior Honor Society

questions.question: Which combination of tools does Makenzie Gilkison use?
options: B. A chatbot, word-prediction software, and a text-to-speech reader
questions.answer: B. A chatbot, word-prediction software, and a text-to-speech reader

questions.question: The feature defines a ________ as a computer program that produces human-like writing or speech.
What weakness in AI assistive tools is noted in the feature?
options: A. They cost too much money
B. They are less accurate for children's voices and handwriting
C. They cannot work without the internet
questions.answer: B. They are less accurate for children's voices and handwriting

questions.question: Toyota's new research site is called ________ City.
The site is located near the city of ________ in southern Japan.
What was the site previously used for?
options: A. A closed Toyota automobile factory
questions.answer: A. A closed Toyota automobile factory

questions.question: Phase 1 of the project covers about ________ square metres.
What is the planned total area when the project is fully built?
The project lead quoted in the feature is Daisuke ________.
Toyota describes Woven City as a 'Living ________'.
Which energy source has Toyota chosen for Woven City?
All buildings in Woven City are connected by ________ passageways for self-driving deliveries.
Which company does the feature mention Toyota plans to compete with on self-driving services?
